name: label
role: Single bounded text label. The foundational primitive for badge / status-pill / tag / chip / dismissible affordances. Variants drive visual presentation through tokens. The element is <span> (default) or <button> (dismissible variant).

role.note: Selects the semantic role. Affects shape, padding, and aria pattern. Badge = small bounded text (often numeric or short label). Status = state indicator (Live / Pending / Failed). Tag = categorical label (often inline with prose). Chip = removable label (often in input forms). Dismissible = chip with explicit dismiss affordance.

tone.note: Color tone. Maps to status tokens (--success, --warning, --critical, --info, --accent) plus 'new' / 'beta' for release-stage labels.

deprecated-from: inlined .badge contract (in hero, pricing-tiers, nav, page-header)
meta.consolidationNote: Cons. 5 promotes the inlined contracts; consumers update their HTML to use class="label" and their CSS rules to reference .label[data-role="X"] selectors.

ariaPatterns.iconOnlyBadge: Numeric notification badges (e.g., "3" on a notification bell) must carry aria-label naming what they count.
ariaPatterns.statusRole: When data-role="status", the label is a state indicator — host page can wrap it in <span aria-live="polite"> if state changes dynamically.
ariaPatterns.dismissible: When data-role="dismissible", the element is <button type="button"> with an aria-label for the dismiss action (e.g., aria-label="Remove tag: Marketing").
